strength_scale.strong: At least one organ or validator computes the relevant property from lower-level evidence and has a negative case proving the anti-axiom is rejected.
strength_scale.partial: The substrate witnesses part of the axiom, but the general formal clause is stronger than current public organs.
strength_scale.candidate: The axiom is doctrinally plausible but lacks a public executable witness.

obligation_pilot.note: Obligations decompose a formal_clause into checkable units bound to EXISTING witnesses already named on the row. coverage_tag (strong/partial/blocked) is intentionally NOT asserted here; it is computed by validator.microcosm.axiom_support_cover. This avoids label-echo (AP-1), count theater (AP-3), and projection laundering. All 12 axioms now expose obligations; AX-4, AX-8, and AX-12 preserve explicit layer-debt caps where current witnesses do not close the stronger formal clause.
obligation_pilot.anti_axiom_rejection_mapping_note: Every required obligation has a source-owned anti_axiom_rejection_mappings row. Rows remain non-certifying unless mapping_relation is exact_obligation_rejection or subsumes_obligation with mapping_verified true from source-owned receipt-level evidence; current rows preserve residual pressure instead of promoting endpoint or row-level coverage.

support_expansion_contract.authority_boundary: Routing binds source inputs and validator/receipt refs; strongest_allowed_claim remains computed by validator.microcosm.axiom_support_cover and is not hand-stamped in this source file.

obligations.predicate: a live-state claim carries value, as_of, basis, and rederive fields rather than a frozen bare value

volatile numeric claims without freshness binding are materialized as a stable blocking code before this axiom can claim full support

rows.title: Executable grammar before doctrine authority
rows.formal_clause: authority(d) implies d in L(G), receipt obligations, and anti-claim are present; prose alone is projection.

obligations.predicate: a doctrine artifact may claim authority only when it is accepted by an executable grammar language L(G)
authority-bearing doctrine carries receipt expectations and anti-claims rather than prose-only acceptance
prose-only standards or grammar passes that overclaim doctrine completeness are rejected as authority

rows.title: Reflexive accountability / no privileged meta-layer
rows.formal_clause: For every Microcosm artifact a, any claim phi carried by a must satisfy the same admissibility relation as external claims.

obligations.predicate: Microcosm artifacts carrying claims are checked through the same admissibility gate as external claims, without a privileged meta-layer
release, assurance, or product-readiness claims are blocked unless their evidence boundary is explicit
receipt body leakage and grammar-completeness overclaims are rejected for Microcosm artifacts themselves
evidence truth-floor mismatch must become a release-blocking source gate before AX-12 can claim full reflexive accountability
